name: Adoption-Contract Correctness — interface.md Discovery, GIP Payload Set, README De-Leak, Existing-Repo Tiers

files_modify.purpose: Correct Section 2 (Discovery) to remediate H4: reorder discovery to (1) FIRST check `.nizam/tools/skill.json`, matching bootstrap.sh's actual `.nizam/` DEFAULT_TARGET_DIR install layout for a bootstrapped consumer (currently this path is named nowhere in the file), (2) a repository-root `tools/skill.json` FALLBACK, retained explicitly for a framework checkout evaluating itself (the document's current item 1, 'Consumer-repo discovery') — this fallback MUST be labeled in the document text with the literal phrase "framework checkout" (not merely implied), so it is unambiguously distinguishable from the bootstrapped-consumer path, (3) pinned-checkout discovery resolving a pinned tag without local vendoring — reconciled with bootstrap.sh's `.nizam/` default so discovery no longer misses every bootstrapped consumer. The `.nizam/tools/skill.json` mention MUST appear, in reading order, before the repository-root fallback mention. Preserve the existing 'Manifest resolution' and 'Failure behavior' items and the rest of the document (Sections 1, 3-6) unchanged except for any renumbering the reordered discovery items require. Bump frontmatter `version` strictly above its current value and add ONE `change_log` frontmatter entry (`version`/`date`/`summary`) naming the H4 discovery-order correction, per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8. No other content change.

Three coordinated edits, no others. (1) H5: correct Section 2 item 2 ('Inject the governance directories') and Section 2.1 item 2 (the `bootstrap.sh` inject-step description) to name all four injected directories — `standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/`, `tools/` — AND `NIZAM.json`, matching `bootstrap.sh`'s `REQUIRED_MODULE_DIRS` plus its `NIZAM.json` copy, replacing the stale 3-dir (`standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/`) description currently present in both locations. Both corrected locations MUST literally name `tools/` and `NIZAM.json` within their own item-2 description (not merely elsewhere in the file). (2) H8: add a new '## Adopting in an Existing Repository' section (inserted after Section 4 Drift Detection, with Section 5 Enforcement renumbered to Section 6, or an equivalent placement that keeps section numbering monotonic) stating: conflict rules for a consumer repository with pre-existing `CONTEXT.md`/`AGENTS.md`/CI configuration (how bootstrap-injected governance content coexists with, or is reconciled against, pre-existing files of the same name/purpose — e.g. rename-and-diff rather than silent overwrite), and the three incremental adoption tiers: Tier 1 docs-standard-only (inherit `standard/` alone, no contract loop), Tier 2 +templates (add `templates/` for consumer-authored `CONTEXT`/`AGENTS`/ADR/etc. content), Tier 3 full loop (the complete contract-first Generator/Validator/Evaluator cycle). The new section MUST literally name `CONTEXT.md` and `AGENTS.md` (by filename) and MUST reference CI either as a standalone word (e.g. "CI") or via the phrase "continuous integration" — not merely alluded to — so the section's existing-repo conflict-rule coverage of CONTEXT.md/AGENTS.md/CI is unambiguously machine-verifiable. (3) Bump frontmatter `version` strictly above its current value and add ONE `change_log` entry naming both the H5 payload-set correction and the H8 existing-repo section, per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8. No other section content changes; Sections 1-3 (Core Directive, the remainder of Pinned-Tag Inheritance, Compliance Verification) and Section 4 (Drift Detection) remain textually unchanged apart from the new section's insertion and any resulting renumbering. [POST-QA AMENDMENT (F-019 rework, 2026-07-08): ALSO correct Section 1 (Core Directive)'s payload parenthetical, which still reads the stale 3-dir '(standard/, templates/, schema/)' and contradicts the corrected Section 2 item 2 and the new Section 5.1 -- either name the full payload (standard/, templates/, schema/, tools/ + NIZAM.json) or generalize the Core Directive so it asserts no incomplete payload list. After this fix, NO stale 3-dir payload enumeration (standard/ + templates/ + schema/ on one line without tools/) may remain ANYWHERE in standard/GIP.md. This clause supersedes the earlier 'Sections 1-3 ... remain textually unchanged' statement for the Section 1 Core Directive payload parenthetical only. Root cause: the original contract scoped only the two Section 2 item-2 locations, leaving a third stale occurrence QA caught as an internal self-contradiction.]

H6 de-leak: rephrase the single `.agent/product_spec.md` Sec 2.2 reference (currently: 'It depends on `standard/` (see `.agent/product_spec.md` Sec 2.2) for the frontmatter and documentation conventions...') to cite a framework-relative source a fresh consumer actually has — e.g. `standard/NDS.md` directly, for the frontmatter/documentation conventions — such that zero occurrences of the substring `product_spec` remain anywhere in the file. Bump frontmatter `version` strictly above its current value and add ONE `change_log` entry naming the de-leak correction, per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8. No other content change.

H6 de-leak: rephrase both `product_spec` references so zero occurrences of the substring `product_spec` remain anywhere in the file: (1) the `00_planning.md` table-row description of 'the mandatory pre-code `product_spec.md` + `feature_list.json` artifact pair' — rephrase to describe the pre-code artifact pair without naming `product_spec.md` by filename (e.g. 'the mandatory pre-code specification and feature-list artifact pair'); (2) the Design Decision Cross-Reference's '(`product_spec.md` Section 4)' citation for the framework's named Design Decisions — rephrase to point to the README/guide DD cards (or the `standard/` concept the Design Decisions are actually anchored to) instead of the product_spec filename. Bump frontmatter `version` strictly above its current value and add ONE `change_log` entry naming the de-leak correction, per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8. No other content change.

non_goals: No CONTEXT.md or README.md edits — the first-contact truth fixes are feature 020's scope

No CHANGELOG.md edit of any kind — phase-close CHANGELOG entries for phase-003 deliverables belong to feature 022; this feature's NDS Sec 4 change-record obligation is satisfied entirely by each edited file's own frontmatter change_log entry
No NIZAM.json, ADR, docs/guide/, or schema/ edits — out of this feature's remediation scope (H4/H5/H6/H8 only)
No edit to any file beyond tools/interface.md, standard/GIP.md, templates/README.md, methodology/README.md, and this contract file itself
No change to .agent/product_spec_003.md — it is this contract's spec input, not a deliverable

No change to the section headers, numbering conventions, or body content of tools/interface.md Sections 1 and 3-6, or of standard/GIP.md Sections 1-3 and 4, beyond what the H4/H5/H8 corrections and consequent renumbering require [POST-QA AMENDMENT: correcting standard/GIP.md Section 1 Core Directive's stale 3-dir payload parenthetical IS within H5's scope and is expressly permitted by this amendment.]

verification.acceptance_test: interface.md discovery names .nizam/tools/skill.json first: grep -q '.nizam/tools/skill.json' tools/interface.md
verification.command: f=tools/interface.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q '\.nizam/tools/skill\.json' "$f" || { echo 'missing literal .nizam/tools/skill.json mention'; exit 1; }; nizam_line=$(grep -n '\.nizam/tools/skill\.json' "$f" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); fallback_line=$(grep -niE 'repository root|framework checkout' "$f" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); test -n "$nizam_line" || { echo 'could not locate .nizam/tools/skill.json line'; exit 1; }; test -n "$fallback_line" || { echo 'could not locate repository-root/framework-checkout fallback line'; exit 1; }; if [ "$nizam_line" -lt "$fallback_line" ]; then echo PASS; else echo ".nizam/tools/skill.json (line $nizam_line) does not precede the repository-root/framework-checkout fallback (line $fallback_line) -- H4 discovery order not corrected"; exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: interface.md retains a repository-root fallback for a framework checkout, explicitly labeled as such (not a false-pass on the pre-existing 'root-relative' substring): grep -q 'framework checkout' tools/interface.md
verification.command: f=tools/interface.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'framework checkout' "$f" && echo PASS || { echo 'no explicit "framework checkout" repository-root fallback label found'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: GIP.md injection lists name tools/ and NIZAM.json in the bootstrap-inject description: grep -q 'tools/' standard/GIP.md && grep -q 'NIZAM.json' standard/GIP.md
verification.command:
f=standard/GIP.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import sys
text = open('standard/GIP.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
def slice_between(a, b):
    i = text.find(a)
    j = text.find(b)
    if i == -1 or j == -1 or j <= i:
        print('could not locate section markers ' + repr(a) + ' / ' + repr(b))
        sys.exit(1)
    return text[i:j]
sec2 = slice_between('## 2. Pinned-Tag Inheritance', '### 2.1')
sec21 = slice_between('### 2.1', '## 3. Compliance Verification')
tokens = ['tools/', 'NIZAM.json']
for name, block in [('Section 2 item 2', sec2), ('Section 2.1 item 2', sec21)]:
    for t in tokens:
        if t not in block:
            print(name + ' block missing ' + repr(t))
            sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: GIP.md no longer describes the inject set as exactly the three dirs standard/templates/schema: ! grep -Eq 'standard/`, `templates/`, and `schema/` (directories|into' standard/GIP.md || grep -q 'tools/' standard/GIP.md
verification.command: f=standard/GIP.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; if ! grep -Eq 'standard/`, `templates/`, and `schema/` \(directories|into' "$f" || grep -q 'tools/' "$f"; then echo PASS; else echo 'GIP.md still describes inject set as exactly 3 dirs and tools/ not found'; exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: templates/README.md contains no product_spec reference: ! grep -q 'product_spec' templates/README.md
verification.command: f=templates/README.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; if grep -q 'product_spec' "$f"; then echo 'product_spec reference still present'; grep -n 'product_spec' "$f"; exit 1; else echo PASS; fi

verification.acceptance_test: methodology/README.md contains no product_spec reference: ! grep -q 'product_spec' methodology/README.md
verification.command: f=methodology/README.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; if grep -q 'product_spec' "$f"; then echo 'product_spec reference still present'; grep -n 'product_spec' "$f"; exit 1; else echo PASS; fi

verification.acceptance_test: GIP.md carries an existing-repo adoption section with the three incremental tiers and word-boundary/filename-anchored CONTEXT.md/AGENTS.md/CI (or "continuous integration") coverage (not a substring false-pass, e.g. inside 'reconciled'): grep -Eqi 'existing repositor' standard/GIP.md && grep -Eqi 'docs-standard-only|incremental tier|full loop' standard/GIP.md

ADDITIONAL (robustness — closes the gap where the C8 structural check alone only fires when a version differs, and a literal-string grep for 'version:' would false-pass on an unchanged version line): each of the four edited files' frontmatter version strictly increased vs its own git show HEAD copy

Each edited shipped .md bumped its frontmatter version above its prior value (GIP.md, interface.md, templates/README.md, methodology/README.md each grep a version line) and every bump is recorded (change_log entry or CHANGELOG.md line): verified structurally by validate.sh C8
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C8\] PASS version-bump-vs-changelog' || { echo "C8 did not report PASS"; exit 1; }; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Validator stays green on the clean tree: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected exact summary line 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (robustness, per the contract-001 Mode-B out-of-scope-edit precedent; AMENDED post-approval to exclude .agent/ per the contract-014/016 precedent, because orchestrator-owned .agent/run_state.json legitimately changes during Loop-1 contract negotiation before the generator implements): git diff --name-only HEAD (excluding .agent/) names only this contract's four allowed deliverable paths, no out-of-scope shipped/tracked file (CHANGELOG.md, NIZAM.json, README.md, CONTEXT.md, or any other) was touched
verification.command: changed=$(git diff --name-only HEAD -- . ':(exclude).agent'); bad=""; for f in $changed; do case "$f" in tools/interface.md|standard/GIP.md|templates/README.md|methodology/README.md) ;; *) bad="$bad $f" ;; esac; done; if [ -n "$bad" ]; then echo "out-of-scope modified file(s):$bad"; exit 1; fi; echo "PASS (modified:$changed)"

verification.acceptance_test: AMENDMENT (post-QA F-019 rework; generalized H5 completeness guard the original verification[3]/[4] missed): NO stale 3-directory payload enumeration remains ANYWHERE in standard/GIP.md -- every single-line occurrence listing standard/, templates/, schema/ in sequence as the governance/inject payload also names tools/ within the same enumeration window. Catches the Section 1 Core Directive self-contradiction QA found.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re, sys
text = open('standard/GIP.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
pat = re.compile(r'standard/.{0,6}templates/.{0,10}schema/')
bad = []
for m in pat.finditer(text):
    window = text[m.start():m.end()+45]
    if 'tools/' not in window:
        bad.append(text[:m.start()].count(chr(10)) + 1)
if bad:
    print('stale 3-dir payload claim (omits tools/) at line(s): ' + repr(bad)); sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
"

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/019-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/019-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/019-verify-12.txt for the twelfth). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

amendments.change: Added "-- . ':(exclude).agent'" to the 'git diff --name-only HEAD' invocation so the scope guard scans only non-.agent/ files.
amendments.rationale: As written, the guard scanned .agent/run_state.json, which the ORCHESTRATOR legitimately modifies during Loop-1 contract negotiation (019-contract circuit-breaker counter + contract_approved history) BEFORE the generator implements. The generator, correctly forbidden from writing run_state.json per AGF Sec 5, never touched it, yet the raw guard flagged it as out-of-scope on every feature. Matches the ratified contract-014 form and the feature-016 'missing run_state exclusion' amendment precedent. Intra-.agent/ role-boundary enforcement is handled by Mode B git-diff review + orchestrator state_correction, not by this guard, whose purpose is protecting shipped/tracked out-of-.agent files.
amendments.dry_run_proof: Raw 'git diff --name-only HEAD' -> {tools/interface.md, standard/GIP.md, templates/README.md, methodology/README.md, .agent/run_state.json}; with exclusion -> {the four deliverables only}. 'git diff --stat HEAD -- .agent/run_state.json' = orchestrator Loop-1 bookkeeping only (circuit breaker + contract_approved/contract_amended history + updated_at).

amendments.target: scope.files_modify[1] (standard/GIP.md) H5 purpose + non_goals[5] + new verification[11] (12th entry)
amendments.change: Expanded H5 GIP.md scope to also correct Section 1 (Core Directive) line ~22's stale 3-dir payload parenthetical; added a generalized whole-file guard asserting no single-line standard/+templates/+schema/ payload enumeration omits tools/.
amendments.rationale: Independent QA adversarial spot-check found standard/GIP.md Section 1 still asserted the stale 3-dir payload (standard/, templates/, schema/), contradicting the corrected Section 2 item 2 / Section 2.1 item 2 and the new Section 5.1. The original contract scoped only the two Section 2 item-2 locations, so verification[3] (block-scoped to Section 2) and verification[4] (regex requiring a literal 'and' Section 1 lacks) both silently passed over the third occurrence. H5's intent is payload-description correctness; leaving Section 1 stale left the document internally self-contradictory and H5 unresolved. This amendment closes the specific defect and the class.
amendments.dry_run_proof: New guard run against the pre-rework tree flags exactly line 22 (EXIT:1); lines 57 and 142 pass because tools/ follows schema/ in-window. Implemented by generator rework (Section 1 line 22 now names the full payload) and ratified by Mode B (2026-07-08).
